annotations.label: Cotangent space

anchor.snippet: the cotangent space,

annotations.note: Mathlib has the algebraic cotangent space `m/m²` for local rings but no dedicated cotangent space on smooth manifolds (only `TangentSpace`/`TangentBundle`).

annotations.label: Cotangent vectors

anchor.snippet: The elements of the cotangent space are called cotangent vectors or tangent covectors

annotations.note: Elements of `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R` are algebraic cotangent vectors; no manifold-level covector type exists.

annotations.label: Dimension of cotangent spaces

anchor.snippet: All cotangent spaces at points on a connected manifold have the same dimension

annotations.note: No cotangent space is defined on smooth manifolds in Mathlib, so this constancy-of-dimension statement is not present.

annotations.label: Cotangent bundle

anchor.snippet: can be "glued together"

annotations.note: Changed kind from proposition to definition — the sentence introduces the cotangent bundle as a new differentiable manifold.

annotations.label: Natural isomorphism from metric/symplectic form

anchor.snippet: The introduction of a Riemannian metric or a symplectic form gives rise to a natural isomorphism

annotations.note: No cotangent bundle on manifolds and no musical isomorphism between tangent and cotangent bundles is defined.

annotations.label: Cotangent space as dual of tangent space

anchor.snippet: the cotangent space at

annotations.note: Mathlib has `TangentSpace I x` for manifolds but does not define its dual as a named cotangent-space construction.

annotations.label: Cotangent vectors as linear functionals

anchor.snippet: elements of the cotangent space are linear functionals

annotations.note: No manifold cotangent-vector type in Mathlib to characterise as linear functionals on the tangent space.

annotations.label: Alternative definition via equivalence classes

anchor.snippet: Such a definition can be formulated in terms of equivalence classes of smooth functions

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have germs of smooth functions modulo the square of the vanishing ideal as a cotangent-space construction.

annotations.label: Cotangent space as quotient I/I^2

anchor.snippet: the cotangent space can be defined as the quotient space

annotations.note: `IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R := (maximalIdeal R).Cotangent` is exactly `m/m²` as an `R/m`-vector space (renamed from the older `LocalRing.CotangentSpace`).

annotations.label: Analogy with Zariski tangent space

anchor.snippet: This formulation is analogous to the construction of the cotangent space to define the Zariski tangent space

annotations.note: This is a comparative remark rather than a formalizable statement; no analogy lemma is stated.

annotations.label: Differential of a function

anchor.snippet: The differential of

annotations.note: For `f : M → 𝕜`, `mfderiv I 𝓘(𝕜) f x : TangentSpace I x →L[𝕜] 𝕜` is the differential at `x`, though it is not labelled `df` and cotangent-space typing is absent.

annotations.label: Differential is a tangent covector

anchor.snippet: is a linear map on

annotations.note: `mfderiv I 𝓘(𝕜) f x` is by construction a continuous linear map on the tangent space, but the tangent-covector interpretation is not spelled out.

annotations.label: Differential map

anchor.snippet: We can then define the differential map

annotations.note: The map `f ↦ df` from smooth functions to sections of the cotangent bundle is not defined in Mathlib (no cotangent bundle).

annotations.label: Linearity of the differential map

anchor.snippet: Properties of the differential map include:

annotations.note: Linearity of `d : C^∞(M) → Ω¹(M)` is not stated since the differential map is not defined at manifold level.

annotations.label: Equivalence of the two cotangent space definitions

anchor.snippet: We can now construct an isomorphism between

annotations.note: No isomorphism between the dual-of-tangent and the germs-modulo-`I²` definitions of the manifold cotangent space is available.

annotations.label: Pushforward (derivative) between tangent spaces

anchor.section: The pullback of a smooth map
anchor.snippet: induces a linear map (called the pushforward or derivative

annotations.note: `mfderiv I I' f x : TangentSpace I x →L[𝕜] TangentSpace I' (f x)` is the pushforward/derivative between tangent spaces.

annotations.label: Pullback of a smooth map

anchor.section: The pullback of a smooth map
anchor.snippet: every such map induces a linear map (called the pullback )

annotations.note: The pullback of cotangent vectors under a smooth map is not defined in Mathlib; `VectorField.mpullback` is for vector fields, not covectors.

annotations.label: Pullback as dual of pushforward

anchor.section: The pullback of a smooth map
anchor.snippet: The pullback is naturally defined as the dual (or transpose) of the pushforward

annotations.note: Without a cotangent-space pullback in Mathlib, its characterization as the transpose of `mfderiv` is not stated.

annotations.label: Pullback via equivalence classes

anchor.section: The pullback of a smooth map
anchor.snippet: the definition of the pullback is even more straightforward

annotations.note: No germ-based cotangent-pullback construction is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: k-th exterior power of the cotangent space

anchor.snippet: -th exterior power of the cotangent space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `exteriorPower` on general modules, but not applied to a manifold cotangent space (which does not exist as a type).

annotations.label: Differential k-forms

anchor.snippet: are called differential

annotations.note: Mathlib represents a differential `k`-form on a normed space as `E → E [⋀^Fin k]→L[𝕜] F` and defines its exterior derivative, but has no bundled type or manifold-level version yet.

annotations.label: One-forms

anchor.snippet: tangent covectors are frequently called one-forms

annotations.note: One-forms fit in the same normed-space differential-form framework (`E → E [⋀^Fin 1]→L[𝕜] F`) but are not given a dedicated name or manifold definition.
